mission.title: Install and Configure Contour on Kubernetes
mission.description: Contour is a Kubernetes ingress controller that uses the Envoy proxy to manage incoming traffic to your applications. Installing Contour allows you to leverage advanced routing capabilities and dynamic configuration updates for your Kubernetes services.

steps.title: Add the Contour Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Contour Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add contour https://projectcontour.io/helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Contour using Helm
steps.description:
Install Contour in the 'projectcontour' namespace (it will be created if it doesn't exist) with the following command:
```bash
helm install contour contour/contour --namespace projectcontour --create-namespace --version v1.33.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Contour pods are running successfully with:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Contour
steps.description:
Edit the Contour deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment contour -n projectcontour
```
Add the following under `spec.template.spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Contour
steps.description:
Ensure that RBAC is enabled in your cluster and apply the necessary RBAC configuration for Contour with:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://projectcontour.io/docs/main/contour-rbac.yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Contour pods in the 'projectcontour' namespace with a status of 'Running'. You should also have configured resource limits and applied the necessary RBAC settings.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add contour https://projectcontour.io/helm
helm repo update
helm install contour contour/contour --namespace projectcontour --create-namespace --version v1.33.2

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Contour release from the 'projectcontour' namespace using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall contour --namespace projectcontour
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created during the installation. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete crd httpproxies.projectcontour.io
kubectl delete crd services.projectcontour.io
kubectl delete crd routes.projectcontour.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'projectcontour' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace projectcontour
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to backup the current state of your Contour installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n projectcontour -o yaml > contour-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Contour to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade contour contour/contour --namespace projectcontour --version v1.34.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Contour pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see that the Contour pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n projectcontour
```
Look for any error messages that indicate configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress resources not being routed
troubleshooting.description:
If your ingress resources are not being routed correctly, ensure that the Contour service is running and check the configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n projectcontour
kubectl describe ingress <ingress-name> -n <namespace>
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues preventing access
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission denied errors, verify that the RBAC roles and bindings are correctly applied:
```bash
kubectl get roles -n projectcontour
kubectl get rolebindings -n projectcontour
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, check the service endpoints and ensure that the pods are ready:
```bash
kubectl get endpoints -n projectcontour
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -o wide
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster with RBAC enabled and the Helm package manager installed.
